summary: Desktop MVP startup failures for local inference and compute-node operator were easy to miss in practice: UI state could remain in starting while actionable sidecar/bridge errors were not visible.
impact: Users observed Start local inference and Start operator as no-op behavior with limited diagnostics, blocking smoke-test confidence and operator registration troubleshooting.
fix: Drained/logged child stderr in Rust for inference sidecar and compute-node bridge, logged malformed stdout lines, emitted explicit inference early-exit errors, added frontend invoke rejection handling to force failed status and visible errors, and added relay error compatibility hints in compute_node_bridge.py.
lessons: Desktop child-process stderr must always be drained and surfaced; startup invokes must map rejection paths into stable failed UI state; and relay compatibility errors must be explicit to avoid silent operator no-op perception.
